\n \n
Amsterdam Juice Lab· Brandbook 2026 ·
\n
\"Cold-pressed juice kiosk with canal-side vibes\"
\n
\n
€9,800Launch budget
\n
6wTo open
\n
12m²Footprint
\n
NetherlandsMarket
\n
\n
\n\n
\n
01 · Positioning
\n

A concept that already proves itself.

\n

Cold-pressed juice kiosk with canal-side vibes Designed for Netherlands retail reality, priced for early entrepreneurs, ready to execute without hired consultants.

\n
\n
€9,800
Total CAPEX
\n
6w
Signing → open
\n
12m²
Minimum floor
\n
Food
Category
\n
\n
    \n
  • Hyper-specific — solves a single customer need, rejects scope creep
    • \n
  • Small footprint — rent stays below 10% of projected revenue
    • \n
  • Predictable CAPEX — every line item verified against supplier quotes
    • \n
  • Trained-in-a-week operations — no prior experience required
    • \n
\n
\n\n
\n
02 · Moodboard
\n

The feeling.

\n

The room you walk into. The first thing the customer sees before they read a single word.

\n \n
\n\n
\n
03 · Color system
\n

Five anchors. No exceptions.

\n

Every surface, label and digital touchpoint picks from these five. Expand only with tints and shades of the primaries.

\n
\n
#3B6A2E
Primary
\n
#D89A1E
Accent
\n
#F5F0E4
Surface
\n
#1A2E1A
Ink
\n
#C8B996
Mist
\n
\n

Primary carries 60% of visual weight · Accent 20% · Surface fills the rest. Ink only for type, never for background.

\n
\n\n
\n
04 · Typography
\n

Two typefaces. One voice.

\n
\n
Display · Fraunces 800
\n
Amsterdam Juice Lab
\n
\n
\n
Body · Inter 400
\n
The brand speaks plainly. Short sentences. No jargon. Every paragraph earns its place.
\n
\n

Never mix more than these two. Never italicize body. Never use all-caps below 10pt.

\n
\n\n
\n
05 · Logo
\n

One mark. Two lockups.

\n

Primary logo locks the wordmark with the category glyph. Secondary is wordmark-only, used where space is tight.

\n \n

Clear space = height of the ampersand on all sides. Minimum print size = 20mm wide.

\n
\n\n
\n
06 · Facade
\n

What the street sees.

\n \n
\n\n
\n
07 · Interior
\n

What the customer remembers.

\n \n
\n\n
\n
08 · Packaging & collateral
\n

Branded touch at every step.

\n \n
\n\n
\n
09 · Signage
\n

The promise, hung on the wall.

\n \n
\n\n
\n
10 · Interior layout
\n

Every square metre, placed.

\n \n

Isometric reference showing typical equipment placement, staff flow, and dimensions for a 12m² footprint. Adapt to your exact premise.

\n
\n\n
\n
11 · Equipment & CAPEX
\n

Where the €9,800 goes.

\n \n \n \n \n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n \n \n
Line itemBudget
Fit-out & finishes (surfaces, lighting, paint)€2,940
Core equipment (primary category-specific hardware)€2,744
Furniture & fixtures€1,176
Signage, wayfinding, branded packaging (first batch)€588
IT, POS, WiFi, payment terminal€490
Licensing, permits, inspections€392
Inventory — opening stock€980
Contingency (5%)€490
Total CAPEX€9,800
\n

All prices are European averages, benchmarked against supplier quotes in Netherlands as the reference example. micro.svita.ai bears no liability for discrepancies with actual supplier prices in your local market — check the latest list via your cabinet.

\n
\n\n
\n
12 · Financial model
\n

Break-even in sight.

\n
\n
€3,430
Target monthly revenue (month 6)
\n
€2,229
Monthly operating cost
\n
€1,201
Contribution margin
\n
~08mo
Payback (if targets hit)
\n
\n

Figures are a planning benchmark only. Local rent, labour, and product mix will shift them. This brandbook is the complete deliverable — no separate spreadsheet is provided.

\n
\n\n
\n
13 · 6-week launch plan
\n

Signing to open.

\n \n \n \n\n\n\n\n\n
WhenWhat happens
Week 1Lease signed, deposit paid, measurements taken
Week 2Concept finalized with landlord, permits filed
Week 3Contractor onboarded, demolition if needed
Week 4Fit-out in progress, equipment ordered
Week 5Interior finishes, signage produced
Week 6Staff hiring, training, suppliers locked
\n
\n\n
\n
Ready.Amsterdam Juice Lab
\n
Cold-pressed juice kiosk with canal-side vibes
